title: helianthemum vulgare subsp. serpillifolium
intro: Helianthemum vulgare subsp. serpillifolium, also known as rockrose, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Cistaceae family and is native to Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The rockrose plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as having antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Rockrose can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy has not been extensively scientifically proven.
